sources.name: Al Jazeera — Global oil demand to plunge amid Middle East war

text: IMF CUTS GLOBAL GROWTH to 3.1% (from 3.3%); inflation raised to 4.4%. Warns of recession risk. Adverse scenario: 2.5% growth. Severe scenario: 2.0% growth. 'Some damage already done.'

sources.name: PBS — IMF cuts outlook for global growth citing Iran war

text: RUSSIA EVACUATES BUSHEHR: 108 of 128 staff withdrawn from Iran's nuclear power plant. Rosatom: attack could cause 'radiological disaster more devastating than Chernobyl.' Kremlin renews offer to take Iran's enriched uranium.

sources.name: Kurdistan24 — Russia withdraws majority of staff from Bushehr

text: HISTORIC: Israel-Lebanon hold first direct talks since 1993 in Washington. Secretary Rubio hosts Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors. Both sides agree to begin direct peace negotiations. Hezbollah rejects talks as 'futile.'

sources.name: NPR — Israel and Lebanon agree to start peace negotiations
State Department — Official statement

text: SANCTIONS ESCALATION: US Treasury confirms 30-day Iran oil waiver will NOT be renewed when it expires April 19. ~140M barrels of stranded Iranian oil authorization ends. Russia waiver also expired. India faces energy emergency.

sources.name: Bloomberg — US to let Iran oil sanctions waiver expire

text: TRUMP: Tells NY Post Round 2 US-Iran talks 'could happen in next two days' in Islamabad. Pakistan pushing second round. Key gap: US demands 20-year enrichment suspension, Iran offers 3-5 years. Ceasefire expires April 21.

sources.name: CNBC — Trump, Iran war, Strait of Hormuz negotiations
PBS — Pakistan proposes second round of US-Iran talks

text: XI JINPING makes first personal statement since war began: 'World must not revert to law of the jungle.' Presents 4 proposals for Middle East peace. China calls US blockade 'dangerous and irresponsible.' Lavrov arrives Beijing.

sources.name: CNBC — China calls US Hormuz blockade dangerous and irresponsible
Foreign Policy — On Iran, China softens its approach
